verdict_reasoning: PMEL is a type I transmembrane glycoprotein predominantly localized to melanosomes (intracellular melanin-producing organelles), not the plasma membrane. The protein body is not surface-accessible under baseline conditions. However, PMEL is a well-established tumor-associated antigen; PMEL-derived peptides (notably gp100) are MHC-I-presented and clinically engaged by TCR-engineered T cells, CAR-T therapies, and melanoma vaccines. Accessibility is via pMHC presentation, not protein body surface anchoring.
